author: Compost Man Unity code: XY
msg:
1. When they released the CLI task, why did they not tell us the expected time scale before calls would start? On that matter, why did they release it so far before jobs came in?
2. When they released the app on Android, why did they not tell us the expected time scale before proof of work would start on Android? 
3. When they released the IOS app, why did they not tell us the expected time scale, or conditions which needed to be met, before proof of work would be activated?
4. What was the outcome of the vote on the split and when will it be implemented assuming it is going to be implemented. 
5. Why haven't they told us the time scale they expect for the start of Scout and Runner alpha testing or told us the conditions that are necessary for that to start?
6. Why do they not tell us when they encounter difficulties, hurdles or other significant problems? I would enjoy following them on the journey and cheering them on. They only seem to tell us when there's really no choice in the matter. For instance, delays in Nigeria, lack of adoption of Jingle by apps. The answer may be something like they don't want to spook the market and that's okay if that's the answer, knowing that would help to manage our expectations of being told anything about strategic issues that they are facing. 
7. What percentage of the rewards we are receiving are coming from outside partners and contracts other than from world mobile?
8. What are the conditions necessary for clients to start paying for the services delivered by the edge Network, by task?
9. What value does the current proof of work task actually provide and to who? Is that information/data currently being sold to other parties other than World mobile and minutes Network? 
10. We were told that Omri was going to be communicating with us weekly. Why hasn't that happened? 

There are 10 questions I have off the top of my head. I would genuinely love to to hear the team answer these in full or at least tell us that these are not the sorts of questions that they are comfortable answering. 

Perhaps others have other questions to add?

author: OSUN
msg:
Sorry. I know I’m new here but after skimming through the past few days, I had to put my two cents in. 
Have any of you ever tried developing a project like this with hundreds of “employees” (because that’s technically what we are now) bitching and sending emails and asking stupid questions?  I haven’t, but I’ll bet you it’s a nightmare. And I’ll be the first to admit that I’m the one emailing stupid questions. 
I absolutely agree that they should be communicating more with us, but I also understand that if there’s nothing to say then there’s nothing to say. 
We need to keep a few things in mind. 
1) This is an investment. Don’t get ahead of ourselves. Take your time. Run the numbers on small samples. See what’s producing the most rewards and what’s not. But be ready for when it’s fully functional 
2) It’s in development. 
I’m a contractor. Things never meet scheduled deadlines and options are going to change. 
3) We literally are employees of Unity. We need to start acting accordingly, and so does unity.

author: James_WM_ENO
msg:
Unity Network is now ready to accommodate international and domestic Caller ID verification testing.

What is CLI Testing?
Caller ID (CLI) verification confirms that inbound calls display the correct and expected caller information as they pass through carrier routes into your device.

This is real-world telecommunications verification - validating route integrity across retail and wholesale voice paths globally.

How it works:
• Opt-in to the CLI task from the Task Tab in the Unity App
• Receive an inbound test call
• You can ignore the call or answer - if answered, the call will disconnect immediately
• Missed the call? That's ok - submit the number you saw on the inbound call at any time within 24 hours

Network Incentives:
From 0.05 UP to 2.00 UP per call, depending on test parameters.

What's ahead:
CLI verification volume on the network is expected to increase as commercial demand scales. SMS CLI verification is coming soon, amongst additional native & opt-in tasks. Opt-in now via the Unity App to start verifying CLI

author: Mat
msg:
These kind of parameters?
• RSRP — enough signal strength so the phone can register and signal the call.
• RSRQ / SINR — signal quality must be good enough for control-plane signaling.
• Latency — signaling messages must travel quickly enough to complete call setup.
• Handovers (during setup) — only relevant if a cell change occurs while dialing.
• IMS registration state (for VoLTE/VoNR) — the device must be registered for IP voice.
• Call setup success rate (core KPI) — reflects MSC/IMS responsiveness.
• Signaling bearer establishment (RRC + SIP/ISUP) — control channels must form correctly.
